items.title: Removed redundant chat loading indicator in Nexior 🔧
Update Extension to Use Correct Relay WebSocket Path in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update connects the Extension to the correct /v1/ws Relay ingress, replacing the previous nonexistent /device path. The Extension has been bumped to version 0.7.4, and an exact endpoint regression test has been added to ensure functionality. Validation shows all tests passed successfully, confirming the stability of the changes.

items.title: Docs(captcha): publish /captcha/tasks poll API on the docs site in Backend 📝
Streamlined Async Create Response in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update simplifies the async captcha create acceptance response by removing redundant fields, leaving only the task_id and trace_id. The HTTP 201 status remains unchanged, ensuring that the response still indicates successful processing. This change optimizes the response payload while maintaining compatibility with existing functionality.

items.title: Update minimum Extension version for compatibility in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update sets the minimum Extension version to 0.7.3, ensuring compatibility with protocol requirements. A new deployment regression test has been added to maintain alignment between the default and manifest settings. The production environment has been patched, resolving previous issues where compatible devices were incorrectly flagged as outdated.

items.title: Enhance Upload Experience with Drag-and-Drop in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces drag-and-drop functionality for all file uploaders in Nexior, allowing users to easily upload files by dragging them onto the uploader interface. The feature includes a dynamic overlay that appears only when a file is dragged over an uploader, enhancing user interaction without cluttering the UI. Additionally, various uploader components have been updated to support this new functionality, ensuring a seamless experience across the platform.

items.title: Fix chat OOM crash caused by reactive loop in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue where sending any message would freeze and crash the chat interface due to a reactive infinite loop. The fix introduces a guard in the Composer's references watcher to prevent unnecessary resets of the file list during uploads, ensuring stability while sending messages. Additionally, debug logs that exacerbated the issue have been removed.

items.title: Revamp Extension Pairing with Canonical Contracts in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the outdated hand-maintained Extension pairing capability list with a new, canonical generated policy capabilities list. It also includes the `wire_contract_digest` in the pairing challenge and binds version, platform, digest, and capabilities into the signed pairing proof. The Extension has been bumped to version `0.7.3`, addressing previous production failures related to invalid capability names.

items.title: Enhance hCaptcha Async Recognition Output in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update synchronizes the hCaptcha async recognition output with the latest documentation behavior. It ensures that the CLI presents the returned task_id in a human-readable format and adds a new panel for submitted tasks when a task_id is returned without a solution. Additionally, focused tests have been implemented to verify the async submission behavior.

items.title: Enhance Automatic Invoice Handling in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update disallows manual invoice applications for Card orders while ensuring automatic invoice issuance through Stripe remains intact. Key changes include the convergence of duplicate Stripe invoice events and the requirement for a secondary webhook signing secret in production. The deployment has been verified with successful order processing and comprehensive tests.

items.title: Enhance Extension Policy Capabilities in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update includes the canonical `policy_capabilities` in the generated Extension contract profile, ensuring that the Extension projection aligns with the single manifest source. A regression test has been added to validate this change, confirming that the Extension pairing now functions correctly without returning HTTP 400 errors. The rollout is designed to maintain compatibility with the PlatformFrontend pairing consumer.

items.title: Introducing Persistent Discord Bot Deployment Design in Index 🤖
items.summary: This update adds a comprehensive design document for a persistent, auto-reply Discord bot deployment service, transforming the existing one-shot skill into a continuous, per-tenant bot. Key changes include the use of a `helm` provider and a new `connector` auto_env source for managing user tokens securely. The document outlines the core design decisions and resolves all findings from the Codex review, paving the way for the next steps in implementation.

items.title: Enhance hcaptcha CLI with Async Support in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces asynchronous support for the `hcaptcha recognize` command, aligning it with the latest OpenAPI specifications. Users can now include the `--async` flag to receive immediate task IDs instead of waiting for the captcha to be solved. Additionally, the documentation has been updated to reflect this new feature, ensuring users have clear guidance on its usage.

items.title: Fix ChatGPT Conversations Freeze on Send in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue where sending messages in ChatGPT caused freezing and out-of-memory errors. The root cause was identified as the persistence of full message histories, including large base64 screenshots, in localStorage. The fix ensures that only summaries are stored, significantly improving performance and preventing OOM errors.

items.title: Enhance Captcha Documentation for Async Mode in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update documents the asynchronous mode for all captcha endpoints, ensuring clarity on how to implement the `async:true` parameter. Key changes include the addition of the `async` boolean request parameter across five OpenAPI specs and corresponding updates to the developer documentation. This enhancement improves user experience by providing detailed examples and billing information related to async operations.

items.title: Clarify Input Fields for Seedance in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the confusing 'Talking-mode' switch and replaces it with clear descriptions for each input field based on official documentation. Key changes include the deletion of unnecessary UI elements and the redefinition of how to make characters speak, ensuring users understand the differences between first-frame images and reference images. The documentation has been updated across multiple languages for better clarity.

items.title: Fix 404 Handling for Unknown Tasks in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses an issue where polling `/captcha/tasks` with an unknown `task_id` incorrectly returned a 500 error instead of a 404. The fix involves returning explicit JSON responses for various scenarios, ensuring that missing or unknown task IDs are handled correctly without affecting existing billing and processing paths. Live verification confirms that the changes operate as intended in production.

items.title: Enhance Async Captcha Recognition Flow in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update extends the async create and poll captcha flow to include three live recognition endpoints, ensuring all solvers can utilize the submit-now/poll-later approach. Key changes include the introduction of a structured result field for recognition responses and improved inline-resolve safety to prevent billing for unsolved responses. Overall, this enhances the consistency and reliability of the captcha service.

items.title: Enhance Fish TTS Credit Estimates in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update improves the Fish TTS credit estimation by deriving byte counts from trimmed UTF-8 text and including the default `s2-pro` model in cost evaluations. It ensures small positive estimates are displayed accurately without rounding to zero and adds regression tests for Chinese UTF-8 byte counts. Overall, these changes enhance the accuracy and clarity of billing for users.

items.title: Enhance Upstream Correlation IDs for Seedance in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the recording of upstream correlation IDs in the BananaRouter Seedance video path, enabling better tracing of finished jobs back to their upstream sources. Key changes include capturing `upstream_task_id`, `upstream_original_task_id`, and `upstream_request_id`, which are now included in the `ISeedanceVideo` result and logged for reconciliation. This enhancement improves debugging capabilities without affecting billing.

items.title: Enhance AuthPanel Listener Management in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue with the AuthPanel by ensuring that stale iframe message listeners are properly removed when login panels close. Key changes include the addition of cleanup logic for both web and native listeners, which prevents memory leaks and potential crashes during user sessions. Comprehensive regression tests have also been implemented to validate these improvements.

items.title: Fix missing deploy script for yescaptcha in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the missing `yescaptcha/deploy/run.sh` script, ensuring that yescaptcha deployments are correctly applied to the Kubernetes cluster. Previously, deployments were built and pushed but never applied, resulting in stale pod images. The new script mirrors existing deployment patterns and includes a rollout status check to confirm successful application.

items.title: Introducing hCaptcha CLI for Easy Verification in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new hCaptcha CLI, enabling users to classify challenge images and retrieve tokens via the AceDataCloud API. Key commands include `recognize` for image classification and `token` for solving captchas with asynchronous support. The CLI follows a consistent structure with other tools in the monorepo, enhancing usability and integration.

items.title: Exciting New Async Poll API for Captcha Tasks in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the new `/captcha/tasks` API for asynchronous captcha task polling, enhancing the hCaptcha service. Key changes include the addition of an `async` parameter to the OpenAPI request body and comprehensive documentation on the new functionality. The migration also ensures that the API is registered with a cost rule of zero, streamlining the billing process.

items.title: Enhance Captcha Handling with Idempotency Guard in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for the yescaptcha async create and poll flow in the Gateway. Key changes include the addition of a new `/captcha/tasks` endpoint that bypasses auto-billing while ensuring token authentication. An idempotency guard has also been implemented to prevent double-charging during retries, enhancing the reliability of the billing process.

items.title: Enhance YesCaptcha with Async Task Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an asynchronous create and poll mode for the YesCaptcha API, allowing users to submit tasks and retrieve tokens without blocking requests. Key improvements include a new MongoDB task store for ownership enforcement and single-shot billing, ensuring that only successful token solves incur charges. The changes enhance the efficiency of multi-solver setups and maintain robust billing practices.

items.title: Final Runtime Audit Completed in Index 🚀
items.summary: The pre-merge Browser Device Bridge audit has been replaced with authoritative merged commits and production rollout evidence. Key incidents and runtime proofs have been documented, while public rollout remains blocked pending further internal validations. All relevant PRs have been merged, ensuring a clean deployment closure.

items.title: Transforming Task Deletion to Soft Delete in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update changes the `/tasks` delete action from a hard delete to a soft delete by adding a `deleted_at` timestamp. This ensures that deleted tasks are retained in the database but are excluded from task-history reads, enhancing data integrity and user experience. The implementation affects multiple worker scripts across various services, ensuring consistent behavior without requiring client-side changes.
